title: Austere
description: Fabric modpack with a little spice.
body:
![Austere Title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3e3edd2597b4626f7a47182d6c498dab8fee2671.png)

> The most up-to-date list of mods (in the most recent version) of the modpack can be viewed on the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/cykreet/austere).

Austere is a relatively small modpack which seeks to revitalise the survival multiplayer experience, by utilising curated utility, optimisation, quality-of-life, cute, and challenge-enchancing mods.

Some of the significant changes/additions, which provide a fresh experience, include the following:
- **Refreshed Movement/Combat**: the [Firework Frenzy](https://modrinth.com/mod/firework-frenzy), [Enhancement](https://modrinth.com/mod/enchancement) and [Combat Nouveau](https://modrinth.com/mod/combat-nouveau) mods make considerable changes to the vanilla combat system, making it a lot more engaging and interesting — view each of their mod pages for an extensive breakdown.
- **Endless Automation**: really needs no introduction, the [Create](https://modrinth.com/mod/create-fabric) mod adds near-infinite possiblity for automation and breathtaking contraptions.
- **Unique Ender Eyes**: the [End Remastered](https://modrinth.com/mod/endrem) overhauls the end grind, by adding 16 unique ender eyes (required for entering the end), discovered through many different locations and methods across the dimensions, some of these eyes can be acquired by: killing the wither (Wither Eye), exploring mineshafts (Lost Eye) and exploring bastions (Cursed Eye).
- **Temporary Graves**: the [Universal Graves](https://modrinth.com/mod/universal-graves) mod adds graves, placed upon a player dying, which holds the player's items for an arbitrary time. The player's items will be protected for a short time, before allowing any other player to loot a given grave.
- _and many more..._

Since Austere is primarily intended for use in a multiplayer environment, it includes some server-side mods such as [LuckPerms](https://modrinth.com/mod/luckperms) and [Plasmo Voice](https://modrinth.com/plugin/plasmo-voice), which can mostly be ignored in client-based or single-player environments.
